data.title: CaixaBank reports FY results
data.description:
• CaixaBank, S.A. press release (CAIXY): FY recurring Net Income of R$15.5 billion in 2025, up 10.4% YoY.
  • Recurring ROE reached 10.7% as of Dec25, an increase of 0.3 p.p. over 12 months.
  • Delinquency Rate of 3.07% as of Dec25, up 1.09 p.p. YoY.
  • Total Loan Origination of R$690.2 billion in 2025, up 12.2% YoY.
  • CAIXA Assets totaled R$2.2 trillion as of Dec25, up 9.5% YoY.
  • Total Loan portfolio reached a balance of R$1.378 trillion as of Dec25, up 11.5% YoY.

data.title: Indonesia to ban social media for children under 16, AP reports
data.description:
Indonesia will ban social media for children under 16, Communications and Digital Affairs Minister Meutya Hafid said Friday, according to the Associated Press.

Hafid in a statement to media said that she just signed a government regulation that will mean children under the age of 16 can no longer have accounts on high-risk digital platforms, including YouTube (GOOG) (GOOGL), TikTok (BDNCE), Facebook (META), Instagram, Threads, X, Bigo Live, and Roblox (RBLX).

The regulation follows a growing global push to limit minors’ access to social media, including legislation passed in Australia that bans social media use for children under 16, as governments worldwide tighten scrutiny of large platforms over concerns about online safety and youth mental health.

data.title: China presses Iran for safe oil and gas passage through Strait of Hormuz - report
data.description:
China is in talks with Iran seeking safe passage for crude oil and Qatari LNG vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, Reuters reported, citing three diplomatic sources.

China is Iran's biggest oil buyer and gets around 45% of its oil from the Strait, one of the world's most important oil chokepoints. About 20% of global oil flows pass through this waterway, with most of these shipments going to Asian markets.

Earlier this week, Iran's government said vessels belonging to the U.S., Israel, Europe and ​their allies would not be allowed to pass through the ​Strait. It did not mention China.

A bulk carrier called the Iron ​Maiden reportedly passed through the Strait after changing its destination signal to "China owner."

The Strait closure functions as commercial deterrence rather than outright military blockade, according to Kpler analyst Alexis Ellender.

"Elevated insurance costs, operational unpredictability, and rising risk premiums are doing the work that physical closure cannot," he noted. "A full blockade remains strategically difficult to enforce given the size of the waterway and the international nature of the traffic."

Roughly 16M barrels per day of petroleum products — crude, condensates, refined products, LPG and naphtha — have stopped flowing through the Strait, Kpler vessel-tracking data showed.

data.title: Maersk temporarily suspends Middle East-Europe and Far East-Gulf shipping services
data.description:
Shipping giant Maersk (AMKBY)(AMKBF) will suspend two of its container shipping routes in response to security threats in the Middle East and Gulf region, highlighting the ongoing disruption to global supply chains caused by the conflict.

Maersk will temporarily halt its FM1 Service, which connects the Far East to the Middle East, and its ME11 service, which links the Middle East to Europe, according to a notice to customers on Friday.

"For the ME11/FM1 final eastbound voyage, we are finalising the timing and vessel details and will update you as soon as this information is confirmed," the company said, adding the shuttle services in the Gulf region are suspended until further notice.

Furthermore, the ME1 service covering the Middle East to Northern Europe will temporarily drop the call in Jebel Ali, UAE and continue to call India and Oman.

Industry analysts warn that any sustained disruption in the area could ripple through global supply chains, particularly given the region’s importance to both oil exports and containerized trade. Shipping companies are increasingly weighing security risks as the broader conflict continues to escalate across the Middle East.

Read here for Seeking Alpha quant ratings for major shipping stocks and their performance so far in 2026.

Other Shipping Stocks: Frontline plc (FRO), Matson (MATX), and Cosco Shipping Holdings (CICOF)

Shipping ETFs: (BDRY), (BWET), (BOAT), and (SEA).

data.title: Stock index futures stable after Wall Street selloff; eyes on key labor report
data.description:
Stock index futures were steady on Friday, a day after Wall Street fell sharply as oil prices surged. Investors are awaiting the key labor report.

Dow futures (INDU) rose +0.14%, S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged up +0.10%, and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) advanced +0.13% to 25,052.45. 

Oil prices topped $80/bbl on Thursday as investors weighed escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices following military confrontations between the U.S. and Iran.

Brent futures were down 0.1% to $85.33 at the time of writing.

The employment situation report will land before the opening bell, and jobs are expected to be up 60K, a moderate showing these days, with the jobless rate up a tick at 4.4% versus 4.3% in January.

Retail sales data will also land at the same time.

U.S. Treasury yields held steady across the curve. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) stood at 3.60%, while the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) reached 4.15%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was at 4.77%.

Top gainers premarket included Sysco (SYY) +3.87%, J. M. Smucker (SJM) +3.04%, and Invesco (IVZ) +2.52%. Decliners included Cooper Companies (COO) -3.37%, Incyte (INCY) -2.29%, and Diamondback Energy (FANG) -2.03%.

data.title: European indexes gain as oil market rally takes a breather
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.29% to 10,444. The Halifax House Price Index showed UK house prices rose 1.3% Y/Y in February.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.49% to 23,928. 

France (CAC:IND) +0.35% to 8,073. 

In other parts of Europe, the Swiss National Bank’s foreign exchange reserves fell to CHF 710B in February.

Wholesale prices in Austria rose by 1.1% Y/Y in February.

France’s official reserve assets rose to €421.57B at the end of February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.25% higher to 606.4, as a pause in the recent energy market rally helped improve investor sentiment. The situation in the Middle East remains unresolved, with uncertainty surrounding the conflict with Iran. U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the U.S. had “only just begun to fight”.

The euro traded around $1.16, close to its weakest level since January 16, as investors weighed the escalating Middle East conflict and rising inflation risks.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area GDP expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.15%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 2.86%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.56%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Bridger Aerospace targets over 25% revenue growth in 2026 as new fleet assets and contracts expand operations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bridger Aerospace Group Holdings, Inc. (BAER) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Sam Davis, President & CEO, highlighted "record operational and financial performance again in 2025." He noted that "revenue and adjusted EBITDA both growing by more than 20%" and the company generated positive net income and a second year of positive cash flow, even as 2025 was a "statistically a below-average fire year." He emphasized increased utilization, nearly doubling multi-mission aircraft flight hours, and a shift in wildfire response strategy among federal and state customers toward early detection and rapid response. Davis stated, "We are directing our efforts to maximize the use of aircraft we have while finding other opportunities to expand our capacity with additional aircraft." The company is targeting multiyear and exclusive-use contracts to build revenue resiliency. Davis also announced a new five-year, $18.6 million IDIQ contract for fixed-wing transportation services in Alaska and discussed active contract talks for Spanish Super Scoopers in Europe.
  • Davis provided updates on acquisitions, noting that FMS contributed $7.9 million in revenue for 2025 and emphasized "technology-enhanced platforms are in high demand and have been instrumental in our ability to position Bridger for high-margin work." Ignis Technologies continues to expand its mobile platform, with new capabilities linking real-time imagery to ground operations.
  • Davis projected, "we are well positioned for another year of greater than 25% growth" in 2026, supported by new Spanish scoopers, Air Attack aircraft, and an improved balance sheet.
  • Eric Gerratt, Chief Financial Officer, stated, "Revenue was $8.5 million compared to $15.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2024." He detailed cost reductions, a gain of $16.9 million related to a sale-leaseback transaction, and a net loss of $15.1 million for the quarter. Gerratt said, "We ended 2025 with total cash and cash equivalents of $31.4 million" and highlighted a new $331.5 million senior secured facility.
  • Anne Hayes, Deputy Chief Financial Officer, announced guidance for 2026: "$135 million to $145 million for total revenues and $55 million to $60 million for adjusted EBITDA." Hayes added, "The company also expects continued improvement in cash provided by operating activities in 2026 and positive net income." She noted the impact of new aircraft and projected 10%-15% of 2026 revenue from Spanish scoopers and new MMA aircraft at a 40% EBITDA margin.
  • Davis announced executive changes: "Eric is officially retiring at the end of the month, and Anne is taking over the CFO role officially on March 10." He introduced Bill Andrews as the new Chief Operating Officer, noting his extensive experience at Lockheed Martin and as a USAF veteran.

Outlook

  • Hayes provided 2026 guidance of $135 million to $145 million in total revenue and $55 million to $60 million in adjusted EBITDA. She expects "continued improvement in cash provided by operating activities in 2026 and positive net income." Contribution from new international aircraft is projected at 10%-15% of revenue with approximately 40% EBITDA margin. Management expects a net loss in Q1 due to winter maintenance activity.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q4 2025 was $8.5 million. Cost of revenues was $14.1 million, with flight operations at $5.7 million and maintenance at $8.4 million. Selling, general and administrative expenses were $13.4 million. Interest expense was $6 million. Other income was $10 million, driven by a $16.9 million sale-leaseback gain offset by a $7.8 million loss on debt extinguishment. Net loss for the quarter was $15.1 million or $0.40 per diluted share. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $9.5 million.
  • Full-year 2025 revenue was $122.8 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $45.3 million and net income of $4.1 million. Cash and cash equivalents at year-end were $31.4 million.

Q&A

  • Austin Moeller, Canaccord Genuity Corp., Research Division: Asked about the appointment of Bill Andrews and potential for C-130 or other aircraft purchases. Davis responded that Andrews' focus is fleet deployment, operational excellence, and leveraging expertise for expansion and defense work, with FMS as a key part.
  • Moeller also inquired about the timeline for Spanish Super Scoopers' return-to-service and potential acquisition. Davis stated the third aircraft is "quite near certification of airworthiness" and the fourth is further out, but focus is on deploying the first two in Europe this year.
  • Moeller asked about potential contract opportunities in Europe and which countries are most likely for deployment. Davis named Portugal and Turkey as leading candidates, aiming to have contracts defined by March or April.
  • Mark Williams, Emerging Growth Research LLC: Asked about normalized EBITDA margins absent return-to-service work and drivers of margin expansion. Davis cited increased utilization and high-margin sensor-enhanced aircraft, while Hayes specified scoopers and MMA aircraft can exceed 40% EBITDA margin.
  • Williams also questioned maintenance expense scaling. Davis and Hayes explained that economies of scale and reduced return-to-service work should lower maintenance growth relative to revenue.
  • Williams queried funding needs given the DDTL facility. Davis and Gerratt stated existing credit facilities cover planned fleet expansion, with about $90 million remaining available.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were focused on future growth drivers, margin expansion, and capital requirements, with a neutral to slightly positive tone. Questions were probing but not adversarial.
  • Management’s tone in prepared remarks was confident and optimistic, using phrases like "we are well positioned" and "exciting opportunities before us." In Q&A, responses remained direct, emphasizing readiness, operational discipline, and financial flexibility.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a confident tone, but analysts in both quarters focused on execution and capital deployment.
  • Management consistently used confident language, while analysts showed cautious optimism about new initiatives and asset deployment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 was newly initiated at $135 million to $145 million in revenue and $55 million to $60 million in adjusted EBITDA, up from the previous quarter’s end-2025 guidance of $118 million to $123 million revenue and $42 million to $48 million adjusted EBITDA.
  • Strategic priorities shifted toward integrating new assets, expanding international presence, and leveraging technology for higher margins.
  • Analysts remained focused on asset utilization, capital structure, and international opportunities, with questions on Europe increasing.
  • Management’s tone remained optimistic and emphasized execution and innovation.
  • Key metric changes include higher revenue and EBITDA targets, more aircraft on balance sheet, and stronger balance sheet flexibility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged that 2025 was a below-average fire year, but mitigation included expanding contract types and fleet utilization.
  • Delays in FMS revenue due to federal budgeting uncertainties were noted, with management reallocating business development resources to pursue defense and nonseasonal work.
  • Analysts expressed concern about scaling maintenance expenses, timing of new aircraft returns, and sufficiency of funding for expansion, all addressed by management as manageable with current plans and credit facilities.

Final Takeaway

Bridger Aerospace enters 2026 with a strong foundation built on record operational performance, new multi-year contracts, and expanded international assets. Management projects over 25% revenue growth, driven by fleet expansion, exclusive-use contracts, and high-margin technology-enhanced aircraft. The appointment of new leadership, ongoing integration of acquisitions, and enhanced balance sheet flexibility position the company to capitalize on evolving wildfire response strategies and growing global demand for aerial firefighting solutions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Medtronic subsidiary MiniMed prices $560M IPO at $20 per share
data.description:
• MiniMed Group (MMED), a diabetes care company and a subsidiary of Medtronic (MDT), priced its initial public offering of 28M shares at $20 per share. The offering is expected to raise $560M.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on March 6, 2026, on the Nasdaq under the ticker MMED. 
  • The offering is scheduled to close on March 9, 2026.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to buy up to 4.2M additional shares. After the IPO, Medtronic (MDT) is expected to hold about a 90.03% stake, or 88.70% if the option is fully exercised.

  • The Minimed Group (MMED) plans to use the proceeds for general corporate purposes, repayment of intercompany debt to Medtronic (MDT), and payments related to assets transferred during the separation.

  • Medtronic (MDT) closed lower at $93.01 on Thursday.

data.title: Headwater Exploration reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Headwater Exploration press release (HWX:CA): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.12.
  • Revenue of $145.31M (-7.1% Y/Y).
  • Achieved record average production of 24,259 boe/d, an increase of 13% over 2024 fourth quarter production of 21,559 boe/d.
  • Realized adjusted funds flow from operations (1) of $79.3 million ($0.33 per basic share (2)) and cash flows from operating activities of $72.7 million ($0.31 per basic share).

data.title: Purebread Brands taps industry veteran Christian Bullock as CEO; C$5.2M debt transitioned to private holder
